candidates.name: Delego-Dev/delego
candidates.title: delego
candidates.description: A policy & audit firewall for agent actions — deterministic authorization, action logging, and policy enforcement for AI agent tool calls.
candidates.signal_summary: Created 2026-06-03. Matches agent security/policy workflow. 6 stars, pushed recently. No license asserted in API metadata.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Addresses the growing need for deterministic authorization and audit trails for agent tool execution — a gap in most agent frameworks.

reasons: Repository created 2026-06-03. License not asserted in GitHub API metadata. Low star count but conceptually relevant to agent security.

candidates.name: stefanoamorelli/inspect-evals-mcptox
candidates.title: inspect-evals-mcptox
candidates.description: MCPTox: a Tool Poisoning Attack benchmark for LLM agents, implemented as an Inspect AI eval.
candidates.signal_summary: Created 2026-06-04. Implements a benchmark for MCP tool poisoning attacks using Inspect AI framework.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: First known dedicated benchmark for MCP tool poisoning — directly relevant to agent safety evaluation.

reasons: Very new repository. No license asserted. Niche security eval that fills a gap in the ecosystem.

candidates.name: ZJU-REAL/ClawGUI
candidates.title: ClawGUI
candidates.description: Build, Evaluate, and Deploy GUI Agents — online RL training, standardized benchmarks, and real-device deployment in one framework.
candidates.signal_summary: Created 2026-04-07, pushed 2026-06-03. 1275 stars. Topics: agentrl, guiagents, mobile-agent, onlinerl, openclaw.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Combines GUI agent training, benchmarking, and deployment — relevant for computer-use agent builders.

reasons: Decent star count for age. License not asserted. Covers an important niche in agent evaluation.

candidates.name: trycua/cua
candidates.title: cua
candidates.description: Open-source infrastructure for Computer-Use Agents. Sandboxes, SDKs, and benchmarks to train and evaluate AI agents that interact with computers.
candidates.signal_summary: Created 2025-01-31, actively maintained. 17661 stars. Topics: agent, ai-agent, apple, computer-use, computer-use-agent.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Established computer-use agent infrastructure with sandboxes and benchmarks. High star count signals ecosystem adoption.

reasons: Well-established repo with strong community. Computer-use agents inherently need elevated permissions — not a red flag but worth noting.

candidates.name: internetyev/cross-agent-memory-kit
candidates.title: cross-agent-memory-kit
candidates.description: Persistent, self-updating memory for AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, etc.) with cross-agent memory sharing.
candidates.signal_summary: Created 2026-06-03. 3 stars. Addresses cross-agent memory persistence gap.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Cross-agent memory sharing is an unsolved problem for multi-agent workflows. This could be an early signal of a useful pattern.

reasons: Very new. Low adoption. Memory access is a sensitive permission category.

candidates.name: IBM/AssetOpsBench
candidates.title: AssetOpsBench
candidates.description: Industry 4.0 unified benchmark for building, orchestrating, and evaluating domain-specific AI agents in physical asset management.
candidates.signal_summary: Created 2025-05-01, pushed 2026-06-05. 1708 stars. IBM-backed domain-specific agent benchmark.
candidates.why_builders_should_care: Enterprise-grade domain-specific agent benchmark from IBM. Shows agent evaluation expanding beyond general coding into industrial domains.

reasons: IBM-backed project with solid star count. License not asserted in API metadata but likely Apache-2.0 given IBM patterns.
